core_payload.content:
# Personal AI Assistant Conversation Ingress

## 1. Role
Personal AI Assistant Conversation Ingress owns the graph-grounded conversation surfaces that let one human speak to the assistant without dissolving capsule discipline. It carries the ingress path from chat into session state and prompt structure so the assistant can understand intent before planning or delegation begins.

## 2. Conversation Surface
This sub-hub groups `chat-to-capsules`, `ai.conversation`, and `ai.prompt`. Together they define the grounded chat pattern, the bounded session shell, and the reusable prompt assets that keep assistant interaction inspectable, replayable, and anchored to the vault rather than generic free chat.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not own long-term memory, planning compression, delegation contracts, or governance posture. Those belong to `memory-continuity`, `planning-navigation`, `execution-orchestration`, and `governance-control`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Personal AI Assistant Conversation Ingress groups the graph-grounded chat surfaces of the assistant: Chat to Capsules, AI Conversation Session, and AI Prompt. It keeps conversational entry, session structure, and reusable prompt assets together so assistant interaction stays bounded, inspectable, and vault-anchored before planning or delegation begins. This boundary matters because the partner surface should feel fluid to the user while still preserving explicit grounding, replayability, and clean separation from longer-lived memory, planning, and control concerns.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during Dream Personal AI Assistant decomposition to isolate graph-grounded conversational ingress from planning, continuity, orchestration, and governance concerns.
